Since Player BLUE starts with no resources, they have no incentive or ability to propose a fair trade unless they gain something from it. Given that this is a one-time game and BLUE has no resources, their best strategy is to accept any non-zero offer, as rejecting would result in losing nothing (since they have nothing to lose). However, RED should aim to maximize their gains.

Since BLUE has no resources, RED can propose an extreme split where RED retains most of the dollars (e.g., 99/1) while offering BLUE a tiny incentive to accept. This is a classic "ultimatum game" strategy where the proposer takes almost everything, knowing the responder will accept a small amount rather than reject and lose nothing.

The optimal first move for RED is to propose a split that leaves BLUE with 1 dollar while RED keeps 99 dollars. This minimizes RED's loss while ensuring acceptance, as BLUE has nothing to gain by rejecting (they start with zero).
</reason>
